title: <div>TYPE describes the artifact category: SPEC, PROMPT, SCRIPT, INDEX, LOG, MAN
content:
TYPE describes the artifact category: SPEC, PROMPT, SCRIPT, INDEX, LOG, MANIFEST, SHEET, MAP, ARC, SEAL.
ID is a short symbolic or numeric identifier. It may be a structured token, numeric index, or hybrid such as LANG, CORE, WORLD-0100, CHAR-0420. The important rule is local uniqueness within its LAYER-DOMAIN-TYPE family.
VERSION is a numeric or date-based marker, such as v0_1, v1_0, v1_1.

6.2 Examples

VISION-META-SPEC-CANON-0001-v0_1 – canonical vision specification.
SYSTEM-ARCHIVE-SPEC-ROOT-0001-v1_0 – archive root specification.
WORLD-MYTH-INDEX-CHAR-0420-v1_0 – character index for world 0420.
WORKFLOW-PROCGEN-SPEC-RUN-0300-v0_3 – procedural generation workflow spec.
CONTINUITY-ARCHIVE-INDEX-LINEAGE-0001-v0_1 – continuity map.
SEAL-SYSTEM-SPEC-HANDOFF-0001-v1_0 – definition of the handoff seal format.

6.3 AI Instructions for Naming

Whenever an AI invents a new file name, prompt title, or module name, it must:

Determine the LAYER based on the artifact’s purpose.
